pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts confirm this path is used for output
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts confirm this path is used for output

Web search for 'NACA 0099' found no documented airfoil with this designation. Standard NACA nomenclature would interpret 0099 as 0% camber with 99% thickness-to-chord ratio, which is physically unrealistic. Not found in airfoil database files (naca0015.dat, naca2412.dat, naca4412.dat, naca6412.dat are available). QBlade v0.96 can generate any 4-digit NACA airfoil numerically, so this may be synthetically generated despite not being a documented standard airfoil.

Confirmed as real NACA airfoil via web search and airfoil database. NACA 4412 is documented with 4% max camber at 40% chord position and 12% max thickness-to-chord ratio. File naca4412.dat present in workspace airfoil data directory.
Task description line 3 specifies 'Pos: 0.0m | Chord: 1.2m' for the root station. Verifier checks for position ~0.0 and chord ~1.2m with tolerance 0.1.
Task description line 3 specifies 'Pos: 12.0m | Chord: 0.6m | Twist: -2 | Foil: NACA 4412' for the tip station. Verifier checks for position ~12.0 and chord ~0.6m with tolerance 0.1.
verifier.py line 36 confirms tolerance of 0.1 is used for checking chord dimensions (±0.1 meters). This is the tolerance value for verification.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves designing a wind turbine blade with a cylindrical root (NACA 0099, which appears to be a synthetic airfoil not found in standard references) transitioning to an aerodynamic section (NACA 4412, a real airfoil with 4% camber and 12% thickness). The blade has a root chord of 1.2m at position 0.0m and a tip chord of 0.6m at position 12.0m. Output files are specified as geometry export to root_transition_geometry.dat and project save to blade_design_task.wpa. The verifier uses a tolerance of 0.1m for dimensional checks. NACA 4412 is a documented, real airfoil, while NACA 0099 is non-standard and synthetically generated by the task.
